observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC Chairman should submit justification why the posting requirement was not complied with and the required documents were not attached to the voucher.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why the voucher was processed and endorsed for payment despite the lack of the required documents.

Henceforth, management should comply with pertinent requirements before payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

We recommended that the MDRRM Officer should exert extra effort to implement during the year all programs and projects identified by the MDRRM Council to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to respond in times of disaster and calamity.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor should adopt strategies to fast track the implementation of identified programs/projects/activities under 20% DF and to   optimally utilize the same for the benefit of the constituents in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Another audit observation on the 20% DF utilization is discussed in Part II No. 9 in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:

execute a written agreement with the Punong Barangays of  San Juan and San Vicente for them to apply for a service connection with LEYECO so that the chlorinator machine will be put in good use; and

issue an order to the contractor for them to undertake repair of the non-functional submersible motor pump. Otherwise, the municipality shall undertake such repair works subject to reimbursement from the contractor of the expenses incurred.

ensure that the projects implemented by the municipality is in accordance with the approved program of works and detailed estimates.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Project In-charge should submit, for the Audit Team’s evaluation, justification on the difference between the quantities of materials used and purchased in the project at Brgy Alta Vista, else, the amount of ₱14,817.00 shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Similar issue was observed on completed projects at Brgys. Pres. Garcia, Santa Fe, Tag-os and Caghanaan, discussed in Part II No. 8 in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and/or Municipal Engineering Office should furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular  No. 2009-001, dated February 12, 2009 to give the Audit Team enough time for its review and evaluation in compliance with laws, rules, and regulations on the procurement process as well as the determination of the reasonableness of the contract cost.

We also recommended that the Inspection and Acceptance Committee should send notice of delivery to the Audit Team within 24 hours after the acceptance of delivered items has been made.
observations.reason: Sampling disclosed partial implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to update and reconcile their records to determine the actual number of lots owned by the Municipality, their correct valuation in accordance with accounting standards, their condition and other necessary information.
require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to conduct ocular inspection and survey of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality to determine their status, their actual possessor, and possible boundary issues.
initiate legal remedies to secure proper division of lots with Tax Declarations shared with the Roman Catholic Church;
issue a memorandum order for the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and Municipal Administrator to cause the registration and transfer of title of the lands acquired by the Municipality;
require the Municipal Assessor to make inquiries regarding the status of the pending land titling application; and
require the Municipal Accountant to record the parcels of land presented in Table 2 and to drop from the books of accounts those that are erroneously recorded in the books such as communal forest and mangroves.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Assessor make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds for the expenses in the processing of the Transfer Certificate of Title.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
prepare the Inventory, Inspection and Appraisal Form and submit to the Audit Team for validation.
provide the Municipal Accountant a breakdown of damaged structures in Canigao;
together with the Municipal Accountant recompute the cost of the damaged properties based on the prescribed formula provided by the DBM as contained in the Manual on Disposal of Government Property; and
require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries after the proper measurement of impairment loss is computed, and recognize loss on assets for totally damaged properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory on the biological assets of the Municipality and coordinate and assess the existence and valuation of the Biological Asset to establish correct balance; and
Municipal Accountant to make the necessary recognition, corrections, and adjustments on the Biological Assets account based on the result of the complete physical inventory and furnish the Audit Team copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers, together with the Report on Physical Count of Biological Assets.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to:
confirm and/ or request the creditors of the dormant Accounts Payables with missing or misplaced supporting disbursement vouchers/ documents to submit their evidence of the claim;
and Municipal Engineer comply with the reports/documents required by the DA RO8 for the release of the remaining fund tranche; and
evaluate the dormant/ long outstanding balances for possible reversion to Retained Earnings in accordance with the provisions of DBM and COA Joint Circular No. 99-6 and PD No. 1445

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Health Officer to submit a written explanation to justify the noted overpayment of SRA and AHDP granted to Healthcare Workers of the Municipality of Matalom;
Rural Health Unit personnel and other concerned offices to submit lacking supporting documents/ records/ orders to the Audit Team for payment of SRA and AHDP and accordingly observe compliance on the documentary requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives to avoid suspension in audit;
Municipal Accountant to ensure that:
all necessary supporting documents are attached to the claims before approving all payments, and
correct computation based on the guidelines set by Administrative Orders and Circulars are considered in determining the amount due to each claimant to avoid disallowance in audit.
Individual recipients, without prejudice to the joint and solidary liability of those who authorized and certified the legality and propriety of the payment, refund the overpayment of SRA and/ or AHDP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team within five (5) days upon receipt of this report the lacking supporting documents enumerated above for evaluation and review of the Audit Team. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted.
Henceforth, ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation and in no case shall a disbursement voucher be endorsed for payment without the necessary supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit explanation for the Audit Team’s evaluation why liquidated damages were not imposed on the projects presented in Table 8 considering that the projects were completed beyond the contract completion date. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the project duration stipulated per contract and exercise diligence in the inspection of completed projects immediately after the reported date of completion to determine whether projects were completed on time, considering Variation and/ or Suspension Orders, if any, otherwise liquidated damages equivalent to 1/10 of one percent of the cost of the unfinished portion of the project for each day of delay should be imposed and collected from the contractor; and make sure that all extension be properly supported with sufficient documents that would warrant the granting of contract time extension to avoid audit suspension.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
submit an explanation for the Audit Team’s evaluation on certifying the correctness of the SWA and Inspection Report despite the noted defects and uninstalled work items/ materials in the projects and non-deduction thereof from the progress or final billings of the contracts.  This is without prejudice to the issuance of notice of disallowance for deficiencies, if warranted;
direct the resident/ project engineers to:
conduct thorough inspection on all infrastructure projects for validation of the actual accomplishment submitted for progress billing to make sure that all materials and works performed were in accordance with the approved plan, Program of Work and Detailed Estimates or Variation Orders, if any; and
prepare Inspection Report accurately showing the actual work accomplished as compared with the contractor’s SWA and make necessary adjustments/ deductions from the progress billing equivalent to the cost of noted deficiency; and
conduct physical count of unutilized materials identified above and, moving forward, make sure that all unused items are properly accounted for.
We also reiterated previous year’s recommendation that Management should execute a written agreement with the Punong Barangays of Pres. Garcia, Santa Fe, Tag-os and Cahagnaan to apply for service connections with LEYECO so that the Chlorinator Machine will be utilized.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish an Inspectorate Team to conduct monitoring and review of the projects other than those who are involved directly in the prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Through the department heads concerned, expedite the implementation of these development projects.
Together with the Sangguniang Bayan, re-appropriate or augment the savings from implemented PPAs to other development projects needed by the Municipality.
Consider the creation of an Inspectorate Committee who will monitor the implementation of the projects by the Municipal Engineering Office.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
derecognize in the books the cost of Concreting of Monte Alegre Pathway to Sitio Opaw amounting to P219,337.00 considering that this is a barangay facility.
ensure that payments should be made based on properly documented obligations and record these transactions in the proper account.
Moreover, we recommended that the Inspectorate Committee to:
conduct thorough inspection on all government projects to ensure strict conformance with the approved Program of Work and Detailed Estimates
prepare Inspection Report accurately showing the actual work accomplished, document any non-conformance, and compute for the loss which will be deducted from progress billings or require the contractor to rectify any deficiencies.
In a written reply dated March 30, 2022, the Municipal Accountant already made necessary adjustments and attached copies of the JEV in his reply.
Moreover, the personnel concerned signified to implement the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Together with the Sangguniang Bayan, plan out the budgeting process to include assessment of a Barangay’s capacity to implement FA projects and evaluation if proposed projects are procurement and implementation-ready as required by DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.
Include in the MOA a timeline on implementation or at least the maximum period allowed after the release of funds for the barangay to complete the implementation and liquidation procedures and the sanctions to be imposed if not followed;
Require the Municipal Engineer to retain copies of Program of Works, Detailed Estimates, Sketch Plans and Inspection Reports of FA projects as well as monitor the status of implementation and require periodic updates on these projects.
Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to long-outstanding Due to LGU accounts; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to trace irregular Due from LGU balances and make correcting entries;
The Municipal Planning and Development Officer, during the exist conference agreed to our recommendation and asserted to raise or elaborately discuss this matter at the Sangguniang Bayan session to obtain or formulate remedies to resolve the long outstanding barangay financial assistance.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Council to ensure the preparation and implementation of the Programs, Projects, and Activities for the LDRRMF and monitor its implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Municipal Accountant to transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the 30% QRF and the 70% Mitigation Fund to Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund Book.
The Municipal Accountant was amenable with the recommendation of the Audit Team.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the MDRRM Officer and the MSWD Officer to submit complete list of recipients of the distributed relief goods for verification indicating therein the type and quantity of items received per beneficiary.  The list must bear the signatures of the recipient beneficiaries; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to demand Sales Invoice from the supplier as evidence of the payment made.
Henceforth, we recommend that MDRRM Officer and/or all personnel concerned to strictly monitor the distribution of relief goods, and prepare and submit said report to the Municipal Accountant for recording.
Lastly, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all government claims are supported with complete documents before these are forwarded to the Municipal Mayor for approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to renew the registration of the eight (8) serviceable vehicles and initiate the registration of five (5) service vehicles operating without LTO registration.
Instruct the Custodian or Driver of the Motor Vehicle to stop the operation of the vehicle with expired LTO registration.
Consider the provision of an adequate garage or shed to house all motor vehicles, including those for disposal.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Keep the records of registration for all LGU vehicles, monitor their expiry dates and schedule the annual renewal of LTO registration in coordination with the Property Custodian or Driver of the Motor Vehicle;
Schedule for repair inactive vehicles which can still be repaired within reasonable cost;
Evaluate the unserviceable vehicles for possible disposal; and in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, follow the disposal procedures for non-existing/missing vehicles, pursuant to pertinent provisions in Item 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, consider the increase of the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer, else she shall be granted cash advances up to the maximum cash accountability only.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require:
the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of PPE by type as soon as possible and submit a complete RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant not later than June 30, 2021, copy furnish the Audit Team;

the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct all erroneous classification noted and reconcile his records with the RCPPE; and

the Property Custodian to maintain property cards for all PPEs to clearly establish or report the correct amount of the PPE and have these reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: New Property Custodian was appointed last June 2021, but there was no proper and complete turnover.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all department heads should conduct a physical count of the unissued inventories as of April 6, 2021 and prepare the corresponding RPCI Report which will be the basis of the Municipal Account to restore back the cost of the unissued items to the Inventory account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should require the designated Property Custodian to strictly comply with the monitoring and reporting of inventories issued, prepare RSMI on a monthly basis and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant which will be the basis for the recognition of expenses in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry to restore the amount in the books. Henceforth, he should prepare the appropriate journal entries to record reconciling items to put the accounts in order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require Floi-Quin Shell Station to submit a detailed billing showing the kind of fuel, date, quantity, price per liter and amount of fuel loaded to support the Sales Invoices; and

For similar transactions in the future:

The Municipal Accountant should examine the completeness and accuracy of supporting documents attached to the DVs before it is processed and endorsed for payment;

Inspection Officer should verify the quantity and specification of products delivered based on actual delivery; and

Property Officer should monitor delivery based on the awarded POs.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor should only approve items allowable to be charged against the SEF;

Municipal Treasurer should require the Supplier to acknowledge receipt of payment with Official Receipt and not to release payment in the absence thereof; and 

Municipal Accountant should carefully examine the completeness of supporting documents in every transaction of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer should ensure that the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 on the specifications for the project signboards to be posted at all project locations of the Municipality are fully complied with in keeping with the full disclosure policy for all transactions governing public interest.
We also recommended that the Property Officer prepare a separate Inventory and Inspection Report for all unserviceable items and submit the same to the Inspection and Appraisal Committee.
observations.managementAction: Separate count and report was submitted, however unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipments (PPEs) were still included in the books and were not disposed.

observations.recommendation: 39. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute an Internal Audit Services Unit in the organizational structure of the Municipality of Matalom and assign personnel therein to perform the functions required by the said office.
observations.managementAction: Securing the copy of the necessary documents and establishing the unit is underway; recommendation is being implemented.

observations.recommendation: 44. The Municipal Accountant should secure a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement and the certificate of turn-over or any document from the DPWH regional Office so as to identify the rightful source agency of funds, determine the correct amount subsidized to the project in order that the validity, propriety and legality of the implementations of the in questioned project be properly justified and for recording purposes of the finished project in the municipality’s books of account.
observations.managementAction: Securing the copy of the necessary documents was delayed; follow-up was initiated.

observations.recommendation:
1.23. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require:
a. the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of PPE
by type as soon as possible and submit a complete RPCPPE to the
Municipal Accountant not later than June 30, 2021, copy furnish the
Audit Team;
b. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books
to correct all erroneous classification noted and reconcile his records with
the RCPPE; and
c. the Property Custodian to maintain property cards for all PPEs to clearly
establish or report the correct amount of the PPE and have these
reconciled with the accounting records.

2.11 We recommended that all department heads should conduct a physical count
of the unissued inventories as of April 6, 2021 and prepare the corresponding
RPCI Report which will be the basis of the Municipal Account to restore back
the cost of the unissued items to the Inventory account.

2.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should require the
designated Property Custodian to strictly comply with the monitoring and
reporting of inventories issued, prepare RSMI on a monthly basis and submit
the same to the Municipal Accountant which will be the basis for the
recognition of expenses in the books of accounts.

3.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry to
restore the amount in the books. Henceforth, he should prepare the appropriate
journal entries to record reconciling items to put the accounts in order.

4.10 We recommended that the BAC Chairman should submit justification why the
posting requirement was not complied with and the required documents were
not attached to the voucher.

4.11 Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why the
voucher was processed and endorsed for payment despite the lack of the
required documents.

4.12 Henceforth, management should comply with pertinent requirements before
payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

5.10. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer should exert extra effort to
implement during the year all programs and projects identified by the MDRRM
Council to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to respond in times of disaster
and calamity.

6.11. We recommended that Municipal Mayor should adopt strategies to fast track
the implementation of identified programs/projects/activities under 20% DF
and to optimally utilize the same for the benefit of the constituents in
accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2017-1.

7.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require Floi-Quin Shell
Station to submit a detailed billing showing the kind of fuel, date, quantity,
price per liter and amount of fuel loaded to support the Sales Invoices; and

7.14 For similar transactions in the future:
 a. The Municipal Accountant should examine the completeness and accuracy
 of supporting documents attached to the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs)
 before it is processed and endorsed for payment;
 b. Inspection Officer should verify the quantity and specification of products
 delivered based on actual delivery; and
 c. Property Officer should monitor delivery based on the awarded Purchased
 Orders (POs).

8.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:
a. execute a written agreement with the Punong Barangays of San Juan and
San Vicente for them to apply for a service connection with LEYECO so that the
chlorinator machine will be put in good use; and
b. issue an order to the contractor for them to undertake repair of the non-
functional submersible motor pump. Otherwise, the municipality shall
undertake such repair works subject to reimbursement from the contractor
of the expenses incurred.
c. ensure that the projects implemented by the municipality is in accordance
with the approved program of works and detailed estimates.

8.14 Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Project In-
charge should submit, for the Audit Team’s evaluation, justification on the
difference between the quantities of materials used and purchased in the
project at Brgy Alta Vista, otherwise the amount of ₱14,817.00 shall be disallowed
in audit.

9.12 We recommended that the:
 a. Municipal Mayor should only approve items allowable to be charged against the
 SEF;
 b. Municipal Treasurer should require the Supplier to acknowledge receipt of payment
 with Official Receipt and not to release payment in the absence thereof; and
 c. Municipal Accountant should carefully examine the completeness of supporting
 documents in every transaction of the municipality.

10.8 We recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and/or Municipal
Engineering Office should furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved
contracts and all the documents forming part thereof by reference or
incorporation within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2009-001, dated February 12, 2009 to give the Audit Team
enough time for its review and evaluation in compliance with laws, rules, and
regulations on the procurement process as well as the determination of the
reasonableness of the contract cost.

10.9 We also recommended that the Inspection and Acceptance Committee should
send notice of delivery to the Audit Team within 24 hours after the acceptance
of delivered items has been made.

11.5 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer should ensure that the
provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 on the specifications for the project
signboards to be posted at all project locations of the Municipality are fully
complied with in keeping with the full disclosure policy for all transactions
governing public interest.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify items which do not qualify as PPE to the appropriate inventory accounts and recognize the expense if already issued supported with Inventory Custodian Slip if still serviceable.
2. We also recommended that the Property Officer prepare a separate Inventory and Inspection Report for all of the unserviceable items and submit the same to the Inspection and Appraisal Committee.
3. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute an Inspection and Appraisal Committee and a Disposal Committee who will implement the procedures on disposal of unserviceable properties prescribed in National Budget Circular No. 425 and COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction:
Reclassification of items was made and expense recognition principle was followed.
Conducted inventory and inspection however, review about unserviceable properties disclosed that the Committee failed to conduct physical count of all properties.
On- going implementation

The Municipal Mayor mandate the designated LGU officials as Project-In-Charge to:
4. implement immediately the projects in accordance with the timeframe provided for in the MOA to return to the Source Agency maximize the beneficial use of the government projects/program and the eventual return of unused funds, if any;
5. prepare the corresponding reports for each of the completed projects and submit the same to the Accountant within one month from completion; and
6. report to the LCE any project/program which cannot be immediately implemented for proper disposition or referral to the Source Agency.
The Municipal Accountant prepared the Disbursement Voucher to initiate the return of the unused/unexpended funds or remaining balances and forwarded the same to the Municipal Treasurer for issuance of checks and remittance to the Bureau of Treasury; submitted the Fund Utilization Report on completed projects to the concerned Source Agency including the check on the return of balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
10. Issue Closure Order to business owners who are operating without a Mayor’s Business Permit; and
11. Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, MMWS Head-Designate and other members of the Local Finance Committee to review the provisions in the Contract of Lease and Water Service Contract and make the necessary revisions thereon especially on penalties of delinquent lessees and consumers.
We also recommended that the MMWS Head-Designate:
12. Continue monitoring the monthly collections of the billed water consumption for the Office and take proper actions on non-paying consumers; and
13. Exercise the right to disconnect service lines for reasons stipulated in paragraph 16 of the Water Service Contract.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer submitted the certified list of the uncollected public market rentals so that the Municipal Accountant can record the transactions in the books of the LGU.
The Municipal Mayor issue a Mayor’s Business Permit to business owners and representatives of other government agencies.
The Municipal Accountant and MMWS Head-Designate were directed to review the Contract of Lease and Water Service Contract and to make revisions on penalties for delinquent lessees and consumers and to continue monitoring and exercising the right to disconnect for non-payment where appropriate.

observations.recommendation: 15. We also recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB, facilitate the immediate approval and passage of a local revenue ordinance for the adoption of the proposed revisions on the rates of fees and charges of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Oversight Committee was created. An audit observation about the Revenue Code was discussed.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order an inquiry and inspection on the current market situation and immediately effect appropriate actions regarding the occupants of multiple stalls.
observations.managementAction: The Contract of Lease was revised to suit the current market situation and to avoid disruption of operations/services.

An audit observation about the 20% DF is discussed in Part II No. 6 of this report.
An audit observation about the 20% DF was discussed and actions are being undertaken.
An audit recommendation was taken. MDRRMF implementation will be oriented to disaster preparedness and management. During the Exit Conference the MDRRMO stated prioritization of LDRRM PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
23. Post the Notice of Award, approved contracts and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS website, in the Municipality’s website and in the premises of the Municipal Hall designated for the purpose.
24. Require the winning bidders to submit all documents which are integral parts of the Contract, including the following:
    • Warranty Security
    • Contractor’s All Risk Insurance (CARI)
    • Contractor’s Safety and Health Program for the project duly approved by the DOLE
observations.managementAction: The Chairman of the BAC posted the Notice of Award and documents in the prescribed venues; the winning bidders were required to submit the required documents such as warranty security. The Construction Safety and Health Program was required from the contractor and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
25. The Municipal Mayor reduce the number of Job Order Workers to ensure that functions of existing regular employees are performed by JOs only where necessary and within the age limit, and deploy only to offices that actually are in need of their services.
26. Limit the hiring of personnel under Job Order contracts to those extremely necessary and within the age limit, and deploy only to offices that actually are in need of their services.
27. Require Job Orders to submit primary identification, undergo interview and evaluate the performance of each to give assurance that those hired have the skills required by the office.
28. Incorporate in the contract the number of man hours, quantity and duration of the work to be performed by the JO personnel and require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports to support payments of wages.
observations.managementAction: Action on the reduction and regulation of JO hires is ongoing; functions are being reviewed to ensure regular plantilla employees perform requisite functions; hiring procedures and documentation requirements were strengthened.

observations.recommendation: 29. We recommended that each participant refund the amount of ₱2,000.00 on the excess payment of registration fees for the seminar conducted by a non-government organization. Henceforth, refrain from attending irregular seminars with excessive registration fees to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Refund was made.

observations.recommendation: 30. We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant withhold the applicable tax rate from the honoraria and allowances given to Resource Speakers and employees of other government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant withheld the applicable tax rate from honoraria and allowances given to resource speakers and employees of other government agencies.

observations.recommendation:
31. Hasten the completion of the closure of the Matalom Open Dump Site.
32. Intensify the campaign on waste segregation; and
33. Make the Bio-composter and the Shredder Machine operational.
34. Assign one person to operate each equipment and monitor their output; and
35. Allocate sufficient funds to procure additional equipment to process the garbage to attain its tolerable level.
observations.managementAction: Closure activities and improvements to the dump site were implemented. Campaign on waste segregation was intensified and bio-composter and shredder operations were improved. Assignment of operators and fund allocation for additional equipment was undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
36. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive formulate a GAD Code for the Municipality of Matalom and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for the issuance of the appropriate ordinance. Also, the GAD Database had been set-up spearheaded by the MPDO.
37. We also recommended that the GAD Focal Person incorporate in their GAD Plan and Budget the establishment of a fully equipped lactation station and ensure the accomplishment of the same.
observations.managementAction: GAD Code had been formulated and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for the ordinance. GAD Database was set-up and the MPDO spearheaded the process. GAD Plan and Budget include establishment of lactation station.

observations.recommendation: 40. Request the Municipal Management to verify remittances in previous years for comparison with Municipal records and request HDMF Tacloban Branch or send a municipal representative to verify remittances and to offset the excess contributions to future remittances.
observations.managementAction: Request to HDMF Tacloban Branch was made and official communication from HDMF was requested. The remittance discrepancy was investigated and excess remittance was refunded.

observations.recommendation: 41. Request the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate the amount for such beneficial program.
observations.managementAction: Request was made and fund appropriation for beneficial program was processed.

observations.recommendation: 42. Reduce the number of Job Order personnel such that existing regular employees perform the functions; ensure compliance with wage orders and labor laws.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation implemented to reduce JO hires and review functions; action ongoing.

observations.recommendation: 43. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property Officer to submit the list of all insurable properties of the LGU to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS for proper processing of the insurance coverage of all insurable properties to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified in case of loss, destruction and/or damages.
observations.managementAction: Property list submitted to GSIS and insurance processing initiated.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify items which do
not qualify as PPE to the appropriate inventory accounts and recognize the
expense if already issued supported with Inventory Custodian Slip if still
serviceable.
1.11 We also recommended that the Property Officer prepare a separate Inventory
and Inspection Report for all unserviceable items and submit the same to the
Inspection and Appraisal Committee.
1.12 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute an Inspection
and Appraisal Committee and a Disposal Committee who will implement the
procedures on disposal of unserviceable properties prescribed in National
Budget Circular No. 425 and COA Circular No. 2009-001.
1.13 The Municipal Accountant in his reply dated April 16, 2020 assured the Audit
Team that they shall comply with the recommendations and shall prepare the
necessary adjusting entries. They however explained that they were not able to
make any adjustments or reclassification on the noted PPEs below P15,000.00 since
such items passed the recognition criteria stated in IPSAS 17. Also, the Municipal
Accountant stated that COA Circular No. 2016-006 does not include the LGUs in
the list of government agencies covered by the said circular.

2.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the LGU officials
designated as Project-in-Charge to:
2.14.1 implement immediately the projects in accordance with the timeframe
provided for in the MOA to maximize the beneficial use of government
projects/program and the eventual return of unused funds, if any;
2.14.2 prepare the corresponding reports for each of the completed projects
and submit the same to the Accountant within one month from
completion;
2.14.3 report to the LCE any project/program which cannot be immediately
implemented for proper disposition or referral to the Source Agency.
2.15 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
2.15.1 prepare the Disbursement Voucher to initiate the return of the unused/
unexpended funds or remaining balances and forward the same to the
Municipal Treasurer for issuance of checks and remittance to the
Bureau of Treasury;
2.15.2 submit the Fund Utilization Report on completed projects to the
concerned Source Agency including the check on the return of
balances, to effect its proper and full liquidation.
2.16 The Municipal Accountant, in a letter dated March 19, 2020, gave several
justification or explanation of the observations noted by the audit team as shown in
Appendix G.3.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal
Accountant an updated certified list of the uncollected public market rentals
so that the Municipal Accountant can record the transactions in the books of
the LGU.

4.21 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
4.21.1 Issue Closure Order to business owners who are operating without a
Mayor’s Business Permit; and
4.21.2 Require the Municipal Treasurer, MMWS Head- Designate and
other members of the Local Finance Committee to review the
provisions in the Contract of Lease and Water Service Contract and
make the necessary revisions thereon especially on penalties of
delinquent lessees and consumers.
4.22 We also recommended that the MMWS Head- Designate:
4.22.1 Continue monitoring the monthly collections of the billed water
consumption for the Office and take proper actions on non-paying
consumers; and
4.22.2 Exercise the right to disconnect service lines for reasons stipulated in
paragraph 16 of the Water Service Contract.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order
creating the Oversight Committee in compliance to DILG- DOF JMC No.
2019-01 dated May 17, 2019, which will review the current Revenue Code of
the Municipality and recommend revisions thereon for submission to the
Sangguniang Bayan.
5.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor, as the Presiding
Officer of the SB, facilitate the immediate approval and passage of a local
revenue ordinance for the adoption of the proposed revisions on the rates of
fees and charges of the LGU.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the contracting party in the
Contract of Lease, order an inquiry and inspection on the current market
stallholders and immediately effect appropriate actions regarding the
occupants of multiple stalls.

7.10 We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, through the
Municipal Planning and Development Officer:
7.10.1 Review the necessity and viability of the projects appropriated in the
20% DF which remain unimplemented and re-appropriate the amount
for other currently needed projects, and;
7.10.2 Include only in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU to be
appropriated from the 20% DF projects which are capital in nature.
7.11 In the letter of the MPDO dated March 23, 2020, he explained that he believed the
expenditures for maintaining the forest trees and mangroves as well as the
resurfacing of barangay roads are proper and in accordance with Sec. 3.3.1 and
3.2.3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1. On the other hand, the purchase of fuel and
oil used by the LGU-acquired heavy equipment was part of the Barangay Road
Rehabilitation project. With regards to the PPAs from 2019 and previous years
which were not implemented, the MPDO explained that they exerted efforts to
overcome their backlogs. In fact, the Project Monitoring Report for the 4th Quarter
of 2019 submitted to the LCE showed that more PPAs have been bidded while other
PPAs were either on-going or physically completed. In addition, on the Barangay
Development Assistance Program (BDAP), some barangay officials still failed to
claim such funds for its implementation due to liquidation problems of their
previous financial assistance. Further, the MPDO appreciated and took positively
the recommendations given by the audit team. When the circumstances already
permit after the pandemic crisis, the Municipal Development Council (MDC) will
convene and propose that the said unclaimed BDAP funds be re-appropriated for
other projects which will have a greater impact to the constituents of Matalom.

8.11 We recommended that:
8.11.1 the Municipal Budget Officer obligate the LDRRMF for expenditures
which are related to disaster preparedness and management only;
8.11.2 the MDRRM Officer prepare a budget for administrative needs of his
Office to be funded from the regular MOOE or Capital Outlay budget
of the LGU;
8.11.3 the Municipal Mayor ensure the completion of a permanent and
disaster-resilient evacuation centers where food packs and hygiene kits
can be stored under defined conditions with good storage practices.

9.9 We recommended that the Chairman of the BAC and its Secretariat:
9.9.1 Post the Notice of Award, approved contracts and Notice to Proceed in
the PhilGEPS website, in the Municipality’s website and in the
premises of the Municipal Hall designated for the purpose;
9.9.2 Require the winning bidders to submit all documents which are
integral parts of the Contract, including the following:
 Warranty Security
 Contractor’s All Risk Insurance
 Contractor’s Safety and Health Program for the project duly
approved by the DOLE

10.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
10.16.1 Create policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity
and propriety of hiring JOs taking into consideration the existing
regular employees.
10.16.2 Limit the hiring of personnel under Job Order contracts to those
extremely necessary and within the age limit, and deploy only to
offices that actually are in need of their services.
10.16.3 Require Job Orders to submit primary personal identification,
undergo interview and evaluate the performance of each to give
assurance that those hired have the skills required by the office.
10.16.4 Incorporate in the contract the number of man hours, quantity and
duration of the work to be performed by the JO personnel and
require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports to support
payments of wages.

11.9 We recommended that each participant refund the amount of P2,000.00 on the
excess payment of registration fees for the seminar conducted by a non-
government organization. Henceforth, refrain from attending seminars with
excessive registration fees to avoid disallowance in audit.

12.8 We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant withhold the
applicable tax rate from the honoraria and allowances given to Resource
Speakers and employees of other government agencies.

13.10 We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:
a) Hasten the completion of the closure of the Matalom Open Dump Site
b) Intensify the campaign on waste segregation;
c) Make the Biocomposter and the two Shredder Machine operational;
d) Assign one person to operate each equipment and monitor their
output; and
e) Allocate sufficient funds to procure additional equipment to process
the garbage to attain its tolerable level.

14.10 We recommended that the Local GFPS formulate a GAD Code for the
Municipality of Matalom and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for
the issuance of the appropriate ordinance.
14.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Planning & Development Officer
spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD Database of the
municipality.
14.12 We also recommended that the GAD Focal Person incorporate in their GAD
Plan and Budget the establishment of a fully equipped lactation station and
ensure the accomplishment of the same.

15.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute an Internal Audit
Services Unit in the organizational structure of the Municipality of Matalom
and assign personnel therein to perform the functions required by the said
office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to accelerate the implementation of projects funded by National Government Agencies and ensure that it is completed on time to achieve the intended purpose for which the funds were released.
observations.reason:
Funds received from NGAs were still not fully  utilized as of December 31, 2018. 
The same audit observation is iterated in Part II No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor improve the operations of Canigao Island Resort by adopting better stringent policies on the use of the island and its facilities in order to preserve its natural beauty, attract more guests and earn more revenues.
observations.reason:
During the ocular inspection conducted by the Audit Team last April 22, 2019 it was noticed that additional cottages were built, however, those were implemented early 2019 and not in 2018. The water system constructed in the island still does not operate and is already delayed and way beyond its completion date which is April 14, 2018.

Contract review on this project will be undertaken and Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance will be issued if the situation warrants.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should secure a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement and the certificate of turn-over or any document from the DPWH regional Office No. 8 so as to identify the rightful source agency of funds, implementing unit, and to determine the correct amount subsidized to the project in order that the validity, propriety and legality of the implementations of the in questioned project be properly justified and for recording purposes of the finished project in the municipality’s books of account.
observations.managementAction: No comment on this matter was given by the Management.
observations.reason: No turn-over was made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  record  construction
materials  and  drugs  and  medicines  upon  receipt  as  Construction
Materials Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory, respectively.

We also recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  designate  a  Supply
Officer who will issue an approved Requisition and  Issue  Slip  (RIS)
upon issuance and require a separate  RIS  from  end-users  for  their
issuances which should be summarized monthly to support the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the  Municipal
Accountant.

We recommended that: (1)  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  members  of  the
Municipal Development Council review the Annual Investment Plan of the
LGU to identify which project should be  considered  topmost  priority
that can be implemented during the year; Re-appropriate the funds  for
those which are not urgent  and  not  viable;  (2)  the  BAC  Chairman
conduct the bidding of projects identified in the AIP,  following  the
schedule  of  implementation  indicated  in  the  PPMP;  and  (3)  the
Municipal Engineer implement all development projects  awarded  during
the year to ensure its completion within the  timelines  specified  in
the contract agreement and utilize fully the 20% DF.

We recommended that the MDRRMO implement during the year all  programs
and projects identified by  the  MDRRM  Council  to  ensure  the  LGUs
readiness to respond in times of calamity.

We recommended that the designated  Program/Project  Coordinators  and
the Municipal Engineer implement and complete the projects  funded  by
NGAs within the period specified in the Contract  Agreement  with  the
Source Agency in order to utilize the funds and achieve the  objective
of the program.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) review the  payrolls
and remittances in previous years to determine  the  amount  of  over-
remittances to be applied  to  future  LGU  shares,  and;  (2)  comply
strictly with the provisions of RA 9679 in observing the maximum limit
of Pag-IBIG contribution at P100.00 per month per official/employee.

We  also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  send  an  official
communication  to  HDMF  Tacloban   Branch   or   send   a   Municipal
representative to request HDMF management  to  verify  remittances  in
previous years for comparison with Municipal records and to offset the
excess contributions to future remittances of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor:   (1)   withdraw   the
Municipality’s time deposit and return the amount to the General  Fund
current account; (2) identify programs that can benefit the  residents
of  the  Municipality,  and  (3)  request  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  to
appropriate the fund for such beneficial program.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.) Reduce the number of Job Order Workers to ensure that functions of
  existing regular employees are not performed by JOs and;


b.) Follow the mandated daily minimum wage  rates  prescribed  by  the
  RTWPB for Region VIII.

We reiterated the recommendation given in AAR 2011 Part II No. 4  that
the Municipal Accountant maintain separate subsidiary ledger/s for the
Market and Slaughterhouse’s special account in  the  General  Fund  so
that the necessary financial statements can be  prepared to aid  users
in decision making.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant record the financial  subsidy
and its corresponding expenses in the MCH books of accounts.

We recommended that the LGU comply with R.A. 9003 specifically on  the
closure of the open dumpsite  and  in  segregation  of  waste  in  order  to
protect the health of its people and the environment.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee: 
Conduct a complete physical count yearly on all properties of the LGU, prepare the RPCPPE segregating the unserviceable properties, and reconcile each item with accounting records. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile its records, at least once a month, with the records of the designated Property Officer & make the necessary adjustment in case of discrepancies.

Also, reclassify the inventory items in the PPE to the appropriate inventory accounts and recognize the expense if already issued.




Further, we recommended that the Property Officer maintain and update the property cards to facilitate reconciliation with the PPE Ledger Cards of the Municipal Accountant.  Also, file a request for disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures prescribed by regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Management conducted a physical count of there property, plant and equipment during the year. However, there are some properties that are no longer existing and custodianship and accountability of such are still to be identified. 
As per recommendation, property ledger cards are already maintained.
observations.reason:
Not all PPEs in the record were physical counted  because some were no longer existing, that is why the RCPPE cannot be prepared and reconciliation in the accounting records cannot be undertaken.

Partially Implemented

The reason on the partial implementation of this recommendation is the same with the reason stated in No. 1




Partially Implemented

Though a conduct of physical count on PPEs was conduct on CY 2018, no Report of Physical Count of Inventories as of December 31, 2018 was submitted to the Audit Team.

Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the LGU books the receivables from the water bills.  Also, prepare and certify the list of dormant accounts receivables and obtain the complete relevant documents to validate its eligibility for write-off.  Submit the list with the proof of dormancy to the Municipal Mayor for his approval.




We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts with COA based on the documents submitted by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Receivables from the water bills were already recorded. However, there is an unreconciled difference in the accounting records and MRWS Supervisor’s records. Reconciliation needs to be done and balances should be adjusted, if necessary.
observations.reason:
Receivables from the water bills were already recorded. However, with regards to dormant receivable accounts,  no certified list of dormant accounts were prepared because according to the Municipal Accountant these accounts are still not considered dormant following the  10- year dormancy period.
NOT Implemented
No request for authority to write- off dormant receivable accounts was made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the perpetual inventory method of recording office supplies procured and record the same as inventories upon acquisition and the related expense upon receipt of the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI).

Require all Department Heads to prepare Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) when items are issued from its stock and
submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) with the RIS to the Accountant for recording in the books.  Also, maintain Stock Card for each item for reconciliation with the Supplies Ledger Card in the Accounting Office, conduct physical count of inventories at yearend and submit a Report of Physical Count of Inventories to the Office of the Accountant and the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: As per recommendation, Office Supplies Inventory are not expensed outright as what is practiced by the Management before and RIS is made upon request and issuance of various items.
observations.reason:
As to Office Supplies Inventory, the recommendations cited were already implemented. However, another concern on Inventory account was observed and discussed in detail in Part II No. 1 of this report.





Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF by implementing fully development projects programmed during the year in the LGUs Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: Of the 58 projects for CY 2018 only 11 were implemented or 18.87%.  Also, of the 85 projects funded from continuing appropriations (this includes the CY 2017 appropriation), only 16 were implemented and one was partially implemented. Leaving 116 unimplemented development projects as of December 31, 2018
observations.reason: CY 2017 and continuing appropriations were not fully utilized during CY 2018 by implementing only 16 development projects. Further, upon audit on the same audit thrust in CY 2018, the same audit observation is noted. This is discussed in detail in Part II. No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC recommend the use of alternative method of procurement only when all the conditions or requisites that were enumerated in Rule XVI of the Act are duly complied with to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Procurement is already done thru public bidding unless the situation requires that public bidding is not practicable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Mayor:
Require the designated Chairman of the Physical Inventory Committee to prepare an inventory report of all insurable properties of the Municipality;

Require the Property Officer to submit the list of all insurable properties of the LGU to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS for proper processing of the insurance coverage of all insurable properties to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified in case of loss, destruction and or damages.
observations.managementAction: Insuring the municipality’s insurable assets were already taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Only the Municipal Building was insured while other insurable properties are still to be insured in the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board, through the Municipal Mayor, approve the use of the Special Education Fund only for purposes prescribed in DepEd-DBM- DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017.  Also, utilize the five percent (5%) Reserved Fund for a specific project or program to get the most out of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Management already utilizes the SEF in accordance with the updated SEF utilization guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in the SEF budget funds for the implementation of programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.
observations.managementAction: LCPC Work and Financial Plan is pending approval of the LCE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO implement during the year all programs and projects identified by the MDRRM Council in the Investment Plan in order to ensure the LGU’s readiness to respond in times of calamity.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, of the total available current appropriations of P3,481,810.00, only P756,883.22 or 21.73% was utilized and  69% of the continuing appropriations of P2,876,922.00 was utilized. This means that the identified projects, programs and activities for CY 2018 and prior years were not fully implemented.
observations.reason: An audit observation for LDRRMF is discussed in detail in Part II No. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the created LCPC to prepare an approved Work and Financial Plan and ensure that the amount appropriated for its identified programs and activities is equivalent to 1% of the IRA received.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was undertaken by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor of Matalom require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Officer to necessary action to comply fully with R.A No. 9003, specifically on waste segregation and closure of open dumps in order to protect the health of its people and the environment.
observations.managementAction: A concrete fence was built around the dumpsite to prevent outsiders from getting inside the facility. The construction of the MRF is still on going as of ocular inspection conducted on April 11,2019.
observations.reason: An audit observation on SWM is discussed in detail in Part II No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop allowing cash advances in excess of the total amount of the payroll should be immediately refunded through issuance of Official Receipt. The excess amount of the payrolls can never be used to pay the succeeding payrolls. In the event that the immediate refund is not possible, the balance must always be reflected in the cashbook until it’s refunded.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer no longer allows the issuance of cash advances in excess of the total amount in the payroll.

observations.recommendation:
Management should strictly follow to the mandated processes in the administration of procured inventories and equipment. Specifically, for the measure to be initially effected, the hereunder courses of actions  are further proposed:

Except when mandated to do so under relevant rules, the Mayor’s Office shall absolutely refrain from taking charge in the requisition, receipt, stocking and release of procured inventories as well as equipment;

If employment of GSO is beyond the agency’s financial capacity, the treasurer shall take charge in performing the functions of the GSO; unless for other reasons the latter officer cannot effectively serve as such. If unfit for the duties of the GSO, another personnel could be designated to perform the said post. 

Requisitions shall be urgently carried pursuant to an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for procurements to be done in bulk adopting in the process the modes of procurements prescribed under RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations; and all records prescribed under the relevant rules to be maintained shall be complied with absoluteness.
observations.managementAction: Acquisition of supplies and equipment were no longer made in the Office of the Municipal Mayor. Further, since the LGU does not have a GSO, the Municipal Treasurer took charge for its functions.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Drugs and Medicines on hand as of December 31, 2018 were not directly supervised by the Municipal Treasurer which resulted to improperly accounted drugs and medicines inventory as of year-end.




Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that if mandated under relevant laws and rules, the required intervention of any professional shall be duly complied with in all project implementations. Said practice is to be adhered to for transactions to be considered valid and regular aside from the necessity of causing appropriate implementation of targeted construction or procurement of facilities. In the instant case and for projects of similar nature, pertinent documentations covering FMR shall bear as well as seal of office of a duly registered agricultural engineer such as plans and specifications to be used for the design construction, test and evaluation of agricultural structures, machineries, equipment, processes and system implemented by the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was undertaken by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical
count yearly on all properties of the LGU, prepare the RPCPPE segregating the
unserviceable properties, and reconcile each item with accounting records.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile its records,
at least once a month, with the records of the designated Property Officer make the
necessary adjustment in case of discrepancies. Also, reclassify the inventory items in
the PPE to the appropriate inventory accounts and recognize the expense if already
issued.

Further, we recommended that the Property Officer maintain and update
the property cards to facilitate reconciliation with the PPE Ledger Cards of the
Municipal Accountant. Also, file a request for disposal of unserviceable properties
in accordance with the procedures prescribed by regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the LGU books
the receivables from the water bills. Also, prepare and certify the list of dormant
accounts receivables and obtain the complete relevant documents to validate its
eligibility for write-off. Submit the list with the proof of dormancy to the Municipal
Mayor for his approval.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor file a request for authority
to write-off dormant receivable accounts with COA based on the documents
submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the perpetual inventory method of
recording office supplies procured and record the same as inventories upon
acquisition and the related expense upon receipt of the Report of Supplies and
Materials Issued (RSMI).
2. Require all Department Heads to prepare Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) when
items are issued from its stock and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and
Materials Issued (RSMI) with the RIS to the Accountant for recording in the
books. Also, maintain Stock Card for each item for reconciliation with the
Supplies Ledger Card in the Accounting Office, conduct physical count of
inventories at yearend and submit a Report of Physical Count of Inventories to
the Office of the Accountant and the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Engineer to accelerate the implementation of projects funded by National
Government Agencies and ensure that it is completed on time to achieve the
intended purpose for which the funds were released.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of
the 20% DF by implementing fully development projects programmed during the
year in the LGUs Annual Investment Plan .

We recommended that the BAC recommend the use of alternative method of
procurement only when all the conditions or requisites that were enumerated in
Rule XVI of the Act are duly complied with to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Require the designated Chairman of the Physical Inventory Committee to
prepare an inventory report of all insurable properties of the Municipality;
2. Require the Property Officer to submit the list of all insurable properties of the
LGU to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS for proper processing of the
insurance coverage of all insurable properties to ensure that the LGU will be
indemnified in case of loss, destruction and or damages.

We recommended that the Local School Board, through the Municipal
Mayor, approve the use of the Special Education Fund only for purposes prescribed
in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017. Also, utilize the five
percent (5%) Reserved Fund for a specific project or program to get the most out of
the SEF.

We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in the SEF
budget funds for the implementation of programs and activities intended for Early
Childhood Care Development.

We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO implement during the year
all programs and projects identified by the MDRRM Council in the Investment Plan
in order to ensure the LGU’s readiness to respond in times of calamity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the created LCPC to
prepare an approved Work and Financial Plan and ensure that the amount
appropriated for its identified programs and activities is equivalent to 1% of the
IRA received.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor improve the operations of
Canigao Island Resort by adopting better stringent policies on the use of the island
and its facilities in order to preserve its natural beauty, attract more guests and earn
more revenues.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Officer to undertake all necessary actions to comply fully with
R.A. No. 9003, specifically on waste segregation and closure of open dumps in order
to protect the health of its people and the environment.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to stop allowing cash advances in excess of the total amount of the payroll. Any amount not paid to the payee/s of the payroll should be immediately refunded through issuance of the Official Receipt. The excess amount of the payrolls can never be used to pay the succeeding payrolls. In the event that immediate refund is not possible, the balance must always be reflected in the cashbook until its refund. We recommend the Municipal Treasurer and her collectors to submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and Without Money Value not later than the fifth (5) day of the ensuing month for review purposes. We recommend the Municipal Treasurer, Disbursing Officer and Accountant to have regular reconciliation of their records in accordance with the existing rules and regulations so as to provide an appropriate financial information to the management for decision making. We recommend the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply Sec. 111 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: The excess amount of cash advances were not yet refunded. No receipt presented as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: Require the management to stop the practice of issuing invalid clearances from fund as well as property accountabilities in the process, safeguard government resources against wastage or loss. Immediate refund of the terminal pay and other erroneous claims shall be directed otherwise all signatories to the source documents covering questioned transactions shall be jointly and severally therefore.
observations.managementAction: No settlement made by the Accountant, designated (when the deficiency was incurred).
observations.reason: ND #2016-003(13) was issued last May 04, 2016 for the unsettled NS #2014-003-101 (13) dated February 05, 2014 in compliance with COA Memorandum dated July 18, 2016 – Issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND) for matured and unsettled NS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that if mandated under relevant laws and rules, the required intervention of any professional shall be duly complied with in all project implementations. Said practice is to be adhered to for transactions to be considered valid and regular aside from the necessity of causing appropriate implementation of targeted construction or procurement of facilities.
observations.reason: NS #2013-001-101 (12) and ND #2016-002(12 & 13) were issued last May 24, 2013 and May 03, 2016, respectively, in compliance with COA Memorandum dated July 18, 2016 – Issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND) for matured and unsettled NS.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the immediate settlement by concerned official through the submission of necessary supporting documents and to require the person liable to refund any excess amount or submit a written explanation for failure to do so.
observations.managementAction: No action had been made by the Accountant, designated when the deficiency was incurred.
observations.reason: NS #2013-001-101 (12) and ND #2016-002(12 & 13) were issued last May 24, 2013 and May 03, 2016, respectively, in compliance with COA Memorandum dated July 18, 2016 – Issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND) for matured and unsettled NS.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to intensify collection of long outstanding receivables, and if possibly, implement the legal remedies available to enforce collection.
Management should take appropriate steps to have the buildings and other properties of the municipality insured with the GSIS.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant  record  the  Infrastructure
Assets in accordance with accounting policies  stipulated  in  COA  Circular
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and record in the books of accounts of  the
LGU all properties of Matalom Community  Hospital  based  on  the  documents
turned-over by the Province  of  Leyte  and  obtain  from  the  said  Office
documents pertaining to the cost of the land and building of  MCH  as  basis
in recording.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books  the  Loan
Agreement entered into by the Municipal Mayor with  LandBank   in  order  to
present fairly the financial  position  of  the  LGU  and  comply  with  the
requirements of the grantor on the first release.

We recommend that the Municipal  Engineer  require  the  contractor  to
accelerate the construction of the Matalom Municipal Hall and demand its
immediate  completion.   Also, monitor closely the implementation of
projects to ensure that the interests of the LGU are safeguarded.


    We recommend that the Municipal Accountant deduct the amount of
liquidated  damages  from  any  amount  due  to  the  contractor   for   any
unjustifiable delay in the implementation of the project.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer and other officials  involved
in the implementation of the  Sub-Projects,  submit  justification  for  the
uncompleted projects, the non-issuance of contract time  extension  and  the
absence of documents to terminate the contract. A report on  the  status  of
these projects should be  submitted  for  evaluation  and  determination  of
accountabilities.


      We also recommend that the Accountant coordinate with other  concerned
offices, such as the engineering personnel and the funding agency,  for  the
retrieval of the required documents to comply with  the  conditions  of  the
funding agency.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor,  through  the  MLGOO  and  DILG,
resolve the problems encountered in the implementation of the  projects  and
mandate the Municipal Engineer to implement immediately the BuB projects  in
accordance with the provisions in the MOA in order  to  effectively  deliver
the benefits of the programs to the constituents.


    Also, we recommend that the  Municipal  Accountant  take  note  of the
completion dates and  final  payments  so  that  submission  of  liquidation
documents of the transferred funds to source agencies  will  be  timely  and
the release of  succeeding tranches, if any, will be facilitated.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  implement  fully  the  planned
projects included in  the  AIP.   The  Municipal  Treasurer  is  advised  to
prepare a cash analysis on the receipts  of  20%  EDF  whether  the  planned
activities have actual cash backing for  the  immediate implementations  of
these projects in the succeeding years.


    We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  require  the  concerned
barangay  officials  to  submit  within  the  reglementary  the  liquidation
reports with complete supporting documents of the prior  year’s  development
assistance.  Proper guidance  and  monitoring  should  be  extended  to  the
barangays in the preparation of these reports, particularly the  documentary
attachments so as to avoid delay in the review of the concerned auditor.

We  recommend  that  the  LDRRMC,  through  the  Municipal  DRRMO,
maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF in the Trust  Fund  by  including  in
the LDRRMF Investment Plan programs,  projects  and  activities  which  will
ensure readiness of the LGU in times of calamity.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  transfer  the  CY  2016
unutilized MDRRMF amounting to  P2,651,886.60 to the Trust Fund under  Trust
Liabilities – DRRM account, if supported with sufficient cash, for the  sole
purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management  activities  of
the LDRRM Council within the next five years.

We recommend that the Local School Board, through the  Municipal  Mayor
as Chairman, include in the SEF budget and implement the same,   only  those
programs/projects/activities specifically authorized in DECS-DBM-DILG  Joint
Circular, with emphasis on the improvement of school facilities,   in  order
to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended  purpose  and  benefits  are
extended to students.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board to prepare a SWM Planwith emphasis  on  the  implementation
of feasible re-use, recycling, and composting programs and other  prescribed
procedures of RA No. 9003. Also, implement the projects  on  development  of
sanitary landfill and construction of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF).

    We also recommend that the  Mayor  provide  the  necessary  support  in
enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials  Recovery  Facility
and  implement  waste  segregation.    Also,   intensify   the   information
dissemination campaign  to  adopt  measures  toward  effective  solid  waste
management at the barangay level.

We recommend that Management intensify its tax collection drive by issuing regular billings or notice of delinquency to taxpayers and to conduct constant personal follow-ups on delinquent ones.  The LGU should avail of legal remedies either administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as provided in the 1991 Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters and Notice of Final Payment were already sent to delinquent taxpayers as provided in 1991 Local Government Code.
observations.reason: Increase in CY 2016 RPT collection was very minimal compared to its actual receivables: Increase in collections: Basic - 103,434.16 SEF – 153,290.75 Actual receivables: Basic – 6,611,875.05 SET – 6,613,179.94

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accountant to revert the unexpended balances of PDAF amounting P139,435.00 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the LGU, and remit to the National Treasury the unused funds in consonance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013 which declared PDAF as constitutional.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended PDAF was returned to the Treasury of the Philippines under Ck. No.761487, 04/28/2016 amounting P4,465.00 and Ck. No. 761488, 04/28/2016 amounting P134,970.00

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the computation of payrolls for any payment should be reviewed by the Municipal Accountant or his representative. The DSWD personnel who is in charge to prepare the payroll should be instructed to use only one standard in the preparation of the same.
We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee accept only original or authenticated copies of the bidding documents. If authenticated photocopies, only the legible documents be accepted for review.

We recommend the concerned municipal officials and MDRRM Council to strictly adhered to the existing rules and regulations in the utilization of the MDRRM Funds. The basic documentary requirements for using the previous year’s unexpended balances of LDRRM funds and Capital Outlays should be complied with and attached to the claim so that suspension/disallowances may avoided.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat to submit perfected contracts and its supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) days after perfection in accordance with COA Cir. No. 2009-001, February 12,2009.

We recommend that the LGU ascertains the actual budgetary allocation of fund before the LCE or his representative entered into a contract.  In the preparation of the AIP we further recommend that programs/activities/projects to be undertaken during the fiscal year should be properly and specifically identified whether it was from the continuing or current year appropriation so as to safeguard misleading information during the audit.
