observations.managementAction: The LGU is currently complying with the documentary requirement as set by PD 1984 and COA rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that programs/activities/projects to be undertaken during the fiscal year should be properly and specifically identified whether it was from the continuing or current year appropriation so as to safeguard misleading information during the audit.

We recommend to the BAC Secretariat to post a copies of the Invitation to Bid, Notice to Proceed and approved contract in the PhilGeps and in the website of the procuring entity within fifteen (15) calendar days from the issuance of the Notice to Proceed of all projects.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal officer to strictly comply the provisions stipulated in the approved agreement. If there are diversions or changes from the MOA, these should be supported with the necessary valid documents to avoid from suspension and disallowance of the transactions.
Submit a copy of notice of postings to PhilGeps of Invitation to Bid, Notice to Proceed and approved contract of this undertaking.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal officer to strictly comply the provisions stipulated in the approved agreement. If there are diversions or changes from the MOA, these should be supported with the necessary valid documents to avoid from suspension and disallowance of the transactions.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal officer to strictly comply the provisions stipulated in the approved agreement. If there are diversions or changes from the MOA, these should be supported with the necessary valid documents to avoid from suspension and disallowance of the transactions.
We recommend that the GAD Plan and Budgets should be prepared in accordance with Executive Order (EO) No. 273, RA No. 7192 and JMC 2013.01.
observations.managementAction: Seminars/Workshops were conducted for the preparation of GAD Plan.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should secure a copy of the MOA and the certificate of turn-over or any document from the DPWH Reg’l Office 8 so as to identify the rightful source agency of funds, implementing unit, and to determine the correct amount subsidized to the project in order that the validity, propriety and legality of the implementations of the finished project in the municipality’s books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already requested documents from the DPWH RO8 related to the grants of P500,000.00, like; approved ObR, SARO, certificati on from the DPWH Accountant as to the grant of financial assistance, except for a copy certificate of turn-over.

observations.recommendation: The BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Accountant are advised to strictly and faithfully adhered to the implementing rules and regulations of PD 9184 The Accountant is advised to re-check the completeness of the supporting documents on payments of the accomplishments of the contractors before they are submitted to the Auditor concerned for review.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is currently complying with the documentary requirements set by PD 1984 and COA rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: The agency head should create a committee to conduct physical count of agency’s PPEs and to include other Inventories and Work Animals. Specifically, the processes prescribed under relevant rules shall be used as a guide in accomplishing the undertaking of physical count which shall subsequently end with report preparation and maintenance of records. This shall include the reconciliation of the results thereof with accounting records, books of accounts and during the year-end, the valuations assigned to the affected accounts per the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Inventory is still on going. Physical count takes time LGU’s first time to conduct the physical count. Currently, the committee is tracing the unaccounted items/units.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer, Chairwoman on Inventory Committee and the Accountant gave assurance to the team on the exit conference last May 20, 2016 to submit the CY 2015 Physical Inventory Reports in July, 2016. However, no report was submitted to the team as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: The LGU should strictly adhered to the required releases of barangay shares from RPT and CTC collections within the prescribed period. The Municipal Treasurer is directed to carry out immediately releases and henceforth, avoid the retention of moneys that rightfully belong to other agencies.
observations.managementAction: Barangay shares of RPT collections were regularly remitted to barangays.

observations.recommendation: Management should practice prudence in the utilization of the funds of the municipality. In all circumstances, payments shall be according to their necessity relative to the rendition of public services and must at all times be for the benefit of the general public.
observations.managementAction: The said communication equipment were properly accounted for in the books of the municipality as per verification of accounting records. Photocopy of Receipt/Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment issued.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly to the mandated processes in the administration of procured inventories and equipment. Specifically, for the measure to be initially effected, the Mayor’s Office shall absolutely refrain from taking charge in the requisition, receipt, stocking and release of procured inventories as well as equipment; 2. If employment of a GSO is beyond the agency’s financial capacity, the treasurer shall take charge in performing the functions of the GSO; unless for other reasons the latter officer cannot effectively serve as such. 3. Requisitions shall be urgently carried pursuant to an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for procurements to be done in bulk adopting in the process the modes of procurements prescribed under RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations; and 4. All records prescribed under the relevant rules to be maintained shall be complied with absoluteness.
observations.managementAction: The LCE did not designate any person even, under the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer to assume the responsibility of the procurements in the LGU. If unfit for the duties of the GSO, another personnel could be designated to perform said post; Requisitions shall be urgently carried pursuant to an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for procurements to be done in bulk adopting in the process the modes of procurements prescribed under RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations; and All records prescribed under the relevant rules to be maintained shall be complied with absoluteness.
observations.reason: Since the LGU had no Property Custodian/General Service Officer, procurement process are currently done per office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the rules and regulations on the grant of honoraria be strictly applied for uses of funds to be appropriate, reasonable and valid. Moreover, for lack of valid basis, all erroneously granted honoraria shall be refunded.
observations.managementAction: This practice was discontinue d by the LGU and justificati on was submitted to the team.
observations.reason: No refund was made as of December 31, 2016 for the unauthorized grant of honoraria in CY 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Heads of Offices that are regularly claiming Transportation Allowances (TA) also claimed gasoline allowances be deducted from their regular claims of the latter allowance.

The Mayor should direct agency personnel concerned to prepare the IIRUP for unserviceable properties so that the necessary inventory and inspection could be conducted, and the WMR when disposing of supplies and properties. These damaged and unserviceable supplies and properties should be placed in a holding area and should not be disposed anytime without the covering IIRUP and WMR.

The LCE instruct the concerned officials to initiate measures/action to enable the municipality to formulate a GAD Plan and incorporate it with its regular activities to comply with the provisions of RA 10147 and Joint Cir. 2004-1 of the DBM, NEDA and NCRFW.
